title: Non-record: BigramHash(4096) + Cosine EMA + LZMA-9
body:
## Summary
- **Non-record submission** tested on 1xH100 (940 steps, 10 min wallclock)
- Built on PR #549 stack with 4 targeted changes:
  - BigramHash expanded 2048→4096 for richer local context
  - Cosine EMA schedule (0.99→0.999) replacing fixed 0.997
  - Earlier late QAT activation (threshold 0.15→0.10)
  - LZMA preset 6→9 for maximum compression

## Results (1xH100)
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Pre-quant val BPB | 1.3628 |
| Legal TTT BPB | **1.4775** |
| Artifact size | 7.9MB (49% of 16MB) |
| Steps (1xH100, 10 min) | 940 |

## Notes
- 8xH100 evaluation pending (budget constraint) — on 8xH100 this runs ~7500 steps
- Artifact is very compact (7.9MB), leaving room for model expansion
- The cosine EMA creates a quantization gap (1.37→2.21 roundtrip) that TTT partially recovers — fixed EMA may be preferable
- Developed using [ShinkaEvolve](https://github.com/SakanaAI/ShinkaEvolve) (Sakana AI) evolutionary code optimization with GPT-5.4 + Gemini 3 Pro as mutation operators

## Test plan
- [x] Verified script runs on 1xH100 with torchrun
- [x] Training completes within 10 min wallclock
- [x] Artifact under 16MB (7.9MB)
- [x] Legal TTT produces valid BPB score
- [ ] 8xH100 evaluation (pending compute budget)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
